rulings.Case Title: FILLMORE MANOR, LLC VS. TALIA KHAYET ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 17, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANTS RICHARD WOOD AND RACHEL AMALIA'S NOTICE OF MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS is continued to December 30, 2021 per the order of the presiding judge. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TERRA VISTA ASSOCIATES, LP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED P VS. BERNARD MANGIONE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 17, 2021 line 5. DEFENDANT BERNARD MANGIONE'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Second Amended Complaint states a cause of action for unlawful detainer based on nuisance. Whether complaint for the sole cause of action for unlawful detainer also states a claim based purely on breach of lease is not an issue to be address on a demurrer to the Second Amended Complaint (Notice of Motion 2: 2-5). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: KRISS MIRANDA VS. YUANCHANG HE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 17, 2021 line 6. DEFENDANTS YUANCHANG HE AND ZHAOYING TAN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in clear and concise language a cause of action for unlawful detainer that is based on (1) termination of license/employment and/or (2) tenancy, before October 8, 2021. Current amended complaint appears to be an attempt at filing a supplemental complaint without leave of Court and removes allegations from the original complaint suggesting an existence of the landlord-tenant relationship. If Plaintiff intends to base their action solely on termination of license/employment Plaintiff shall allege facts showing why the change was made compared to the original complaint which referenced both the SFRO and TPA 2019, which are only applicable to tenants. If Plaintiff intends to base their complaint on a termination of license/employment after October 8, 2021, Plaintiff shall file a proper motion for leave to file a supplemental complaint. Finally, it appears based on the arguments raised in opposition that Plaintiff believes that some of the allegations in the FAC are not material and are irrelevant, if so, such allegations shall not be included in any amended/supplemental complaint to avoid any uncertainty. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LE LAN VS. CARLOS SANDERS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 17, 2021 line 12. DEFENDANT CARLOS SANDERS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint states a cause of unlawful detainer and in not uncertain. Plaintiff alleges that TPA 2019 does not apply because SFRO applies. There is nothing uncertain about this allegation. Defendant cites no authority for the proposition that the use of optional form complaint precludes Plaintiff from adding allegations that do not strictly fit into the check boxes. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
